mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically lifting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 4.5 percent within a single quarter, it signals a rapid deterioration in labor market conditions that triggers recession fears, compressing equity multiples and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60 percent of triggered instances despite the unemployment threshold breach.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous contraction in industrial production and a sharp decline in retail sales indicates a supply-demand mismatch that erodes corporate profit margins, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns with a directional accuracy exceeding 55 percent over a 3-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of active central bank tightening, a month-over-month increase in the consumer price index accelerates real rate expectations, forcing equity risk premiums higher and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain statistically indistinguishable from zero during confirmed tightening phases, or the signal produces positive returns in more than 50 percent of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real GDP indicates expanding aggregate demand that supports corporate revenue growth, mechanically driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days regardless of short-term volatility.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in expansionary quarters are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in housing starts exceeding 10 percent signals a rapid cooling in the construction sector and consumer confidence, triggering a flight to safety that drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60 percent of triggered instances despite the housing decline threshold.

mechanism_narrative: A rising unemployment rate combined with a federal funds rate above 3.5 percent indicates a policy-induced labor market cooling that suppresses equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns with a directional accuracy exceeding 55 percent over a 3-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: During supply chain normalization phases, a sustained increase in the industrial production index alongside stable retail sales indicates recovering capacity utilization, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain statistically indistinguishable from zero during confirmed recovery phases, or the signal produces negative returns in more than 50 percent of observations.
